title: CIBF 2026: World's Largest Battery Industry Fair Announced for Shenzhen — May 13–15
detail: The 18th China International Battery Fair (CIBF 2026) was officially announced on April 29 with full exhibitor details, confirming the event will run May 13–15 at the Shenzhen World Exhibition and Convention Center. CIBF is the world's largest dedicated battery industry trade event, bringing together over 1,500 exhibitors and tens of thousands of industry visitors annually. The 2026 edition will feature dedicated pavilions for solid-state batteries, sodium-ion cells, lithium iron phosphate for grid storage, smart manufacturing automation lines, and carbon-footprint tracking systems required under the EU Battery Regulation. Major exhibitors include CATL, BYD, EVE Energy, CALB, and leading international suppliers of electrolytes, separators, cathode active materials, and battery management systems. The event serves as an annual barometer for the global battery industry's commercial trajectory.

media.caption: CIBF 2026 — world's largest battery fair opens May 13–15 in Shenzhen with solid-state, Na-ion, and grid storage focus

title: CATL Declares Sodium-Ion Batteries Ready for Mass Production in 2026, Targeting 600 km EV Range
detail: In a separate announcement tracked on April 29, CATL formally stated that its sodium-ion battery is entering mass production in 2026 and set a target of 600 km CLTC range for future Na-ion-powered EVs using its AB-pack architecture (combining sodium-ion and lithium-ion cells in a single pack). CATL's second-generation Na-ion cell achieves approximately 200 Wh/kg — up from ~160 Wh/kg in the first generation — and the company said manufacturing cost is now within 5% of comparable LFP cells at volume. CATL's Na-ion chemistry uses Prussian blue analogue (PBA) cathodes with hard carbon anodes, avoiding lithium, cobalt, and nickel entirely. The declaration comes on the heels of the 60 GWh HyperStrong grid storage deal and positions CATL as the first major battery maker to commit both to grid-scale and automotive Na-ion volumes simultaneously in the same year.

media.caption: CATL announces sodium-ion battery mass production for 2026, targeting 600 km EV range with AB-pack architecture
